target: Schur-tiling soundness (Half 1) for the deferred large-block-PSD path
reading: The once-proved Haynsworth/block-LDLᵀ soundness lemma the Schur-tiling large-block-PSD path needs: congruence + sum preserve PSD, so an order-N block that is a sum of congruences of ≤60-order PSD pivots is PSD — reducing it to the small pivots + one kernel-recomputed (fail-closed) identity, no Schur-iff. Pure Mathlib theorem, axiom-clean; NO trust surface (it would be used by a future operator-gated tiling primitive). A down-payment on ADR 0047 Option 3's tractable Half 1.
